title: Single Message Transform (SMT) Pipeline
interaction_flow: TransformState → SourceRecord: new SourceRecord(topic='top1', partition=1, schema, value='d') [delete]
TransformState → SourceRecord: new SourceRecord(topic='top1', partition=1, schema, value='c') [create]
NewRecord → SourceRecord.newRecord(topic, partition, keySchema, key, valueSchema, value, timestamp, headers): returns new SourceRecord copy
JMH Runner → SmtOverheadPerf.base(state): [Benchmark baseline — empty body]
JMH Runner → TransformState(FilterSmtPerf).doSetup(): build Schema, SourceRecords (delete op='d', create op='c')
TransformState → Filter: new Filter<>() as groovyFilter; configure({language='jsr223.groovy', condition='value.op == 'd''})
TransformState → Filter: new Filter<>() as jsFilter; configure({language='jsr223.graal.js', condition='value.get(op) == d'})
TransformState → Filter: new Filter<>() as chicoryFilter; configure({language='wasm.chicory', condition=<wasm file path>})
TransformState → Filter: new Filter<>() as chicoryInterpreterFilter; configure({language='wasm.chicory-interpreter', condition=<wasm file path>})
FilterSmtPerf → NativeFilter.apply(state.create): record.value() != null → Envelope op='c' != 'd' → return record [pass]
FilterSmtPerf → NativeFilter.apply(state.delete): op='d' == DELETE.code() → return null [filtered out]
ScriptingTransformation: check topicPattern (null → skip) → call doApply(record)
ScriptingEngine (Jsr223Engine/GraalJsEngine/WasmEngine) → evaluate condition expression against record
Filter.doApply: result==true ? return record : return null
FilterSmtPerf → Filter.apply(state.delete): condition evaluates true for op='d' → return null [filtered]
key_relationships: NewRecord implements Transformation<SourceRecord>, Versioned (inner class of SmtOverheadPerf)
NoOp implements Transformation<SourceRecord>, Versioned (inner class of SmtOverheadPerf)
NativeFilter implements Transformation<SourceRecord>, Versioned (inner class of FilterSmtPerf)
ScriptingTransformation delegates to ScriptingEngine (Jsr223Engine | GraalJsEngine | WasmEngine) selected by 'language' config
Filter.doApply → engine.eval(record, Boolean.class) → returns record if true, null if false
NativeFilter directly reads Envelope.Operation.DELETE.code() to check op field
TransformState (JMH @State(Scope.Thread)) owns all transform instances and test SourceRecords
SmtManager<R> provides isValidEnvelope(), isValidSchemaChange(), isValidHeartBeat() validation helpers used by other SMTs

filter_decision_logic.NativeFilter: if value==null → pass; if op==DELETE.code() → return null (drop); else → pass
filter_decision_logic.Filter (scripting): engine.eval(condition, record) → Boolean; true → drop (return null); false → pass (return record)
filter_decision_logic.NewRecord: always returns a new SourceRecord copy with same fields

scripting_engine_variants: Jsr223Engine: language=jsr223.groovy or jsr223.graal.js — evaluates JSR-223 scripts
WasmEngine: language=wasm.chicory or wasm.chicory-interpreter — loads .wasm file as condition
important_notes: Both benchmarks use JMH @State(Scope.Thread). FilterSmtPerf applies each filter 3x per benchmark invocation (create, create, delete). NativeFilter uses direct Java op-code check; Filter uses pluggable scripting engine. SmtManager is NOT used in these benchmarks — it is the helper for other production SMTs (ExtractNewRecordState, PartitionRouting, etc.) to validate envelope schema before transforming.
